steps.title: Spot the grammatical demand
steps.content: The phrase "including its many ______" requires plural nouns to identify the geological features the lander explored. We're naming the actual features—craters and rocks—not properties or possessions.

steps.title: Apply the plural rule
steps.content:
The phrase "including its many ___" lists types of geological features; the blanks must be plural nouns, not possessives (possessives would need something to possess).
  • Choice A — "craters and rock's": "exploring...its many craters and rock's." — "rock's" is possessive; there is nothing following it that rock owns. ✗
  • Choice B — "craters and rocks": "exploring...its many craters and rocks." — both are simple plurals naming the features. ✓
  • Choice C — "crater's and rocks": "exploring...its many crater's and rocks." — "crater's" is possessive with nothing to possess. ✗
  • Choice D — "crater's and rock's": "exploring...its many crater's and rock's." — both are possessives; neither makes sense here. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
